paragraph: The single-family market in Allendale is a real seller's market right now. Twenty-two homes sold over the last six months at a median of $953,000, with nearly 60 percent selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.034. Homes are moving fast, sitting a median of 12.5 days on market before going under contract. Sellers are dictating terms, and priced homes are drawing multiple offers. The condo and townhouse tier, by contrast, is even hotter. Six sales closed at a median of $794,950, with over 83 percent selling above asking and a median of 11 days on market. The ratio here climbs to 1.075, meaning buyers are paying noticeably above list price to land units in this category. If you're selling, either tier gives you real leverage right now. If you're buying, you'll need to move quickly on anything that fits your needs and come in prepared to bid above asking. Priced competitively, homes don't last long in Allendale.
